candidates.name: Kathy Hochul
candidates.notes: Became governor August 2021 after Cuomo resignation, elected 2022. First woman governor of NY. Signed Concealed Carry Improvement Act after NYSRPA v. Bruen. Strengthened NY abortion protections. Signed NY DREAM Act expansion. Signed Equality Amendment. Former Congress member (2011-2013) had moderate record but shifted left as governor.

profile.education: Syracuse University (BA); Catholic University of America (JD)
profile.background: Attorney and public servant; held various local offices and served as U.S. Representative; became New York's first female governor and 57th governor in 2021.

candidates.name: Chuck Schumer
candidates.notes: Senator since 1999. Senate Minority Leader. Heritage Action 0% current session, 5% lifetime. NRA F rated — led Senate gun control efforts. Pro-choice champion. Supports DREAM Act and comprehensive immigration reform. Led effort to codify same-sex marriage (Respect for Marriage Act). Highest-ranking Jewish lawmaker in US history.

profile.background: Senate Minority Leader (formerly Majority Leader). Former U.S. House member for 18 years. Former New York State Assemblyman. In Senate since 1999. Son of an exterminator and homemaker.
profile.office: 322 Hart Senate Office Building

district_offices.address: 1 Clinton Ave., Room 827, Leo O'Brien Building

candidates.name: Kirsten Gillibrand
candidates.notes: Senator since 2009 (appointed, elected 2012). Heritage Action 0% current, 6% lifetime. Former NRA A-rated House member from upstate NY who flipped to F after joining Senate. Pro-choice. Supports assault weapons ban. Voted for Respect for Marriage Act. Supports DREAM Act. Catholic who attends bipartisan Senate Bible study but breaks from Church on abortion and marriage.

profile.education: Dartmouth College (BA Asian Studies), UCLA School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Former attorney at Davis Polk & Wardwell. HUD special counsel in Clinton administration. Appointed to Senate in 2009 to fill Hillary Clinton's seat. 2020 presidential candidate.
profile.office: 478 Russell Senate Office Building
